metadata.description: An executable has been loaded into memory.

securityResult.description: A process has been denied execution.

metadata.description: An executable has been loaded into memory.

securityResult.summary: A common ZSH syntax was used to create a shell with an outgoing network connection. A reverse shell connection is a way for an attacker to call back to a malicious server from a compromised system. This is often done once the attacker has already gained access to a system and needs to establish an impromptu interactive shell.

metadata.description: An executable has been loaded into memory.

securityResult.description: A process has been denied execution.

metadata.description: The sending of a signal to a process.

securityResult.summary: A user or program attempted to modify the file flags on Jamf Protect.

metadata.description: The setting of a file's flags by a process.

securityResult.summary: A user or program attempted to modify the file flags on Jamf Protect.

metadata.description: Apple's Background Task Manager notified that an item has been added.

securityResult.summary: A Launch Item was created that references an executable located in a non-standard or user-controlled directory, or uses a system binary that is not typically configured for persistent execution. This behavior may indicate an attempt to execute or maintain persistence using an unexpected or potentially malicious method.
securityResult.description: Launch item created with suspicious executable path

metadata.description: A screen sharing session has attached to a graphical session.

securityResult.summary: A screen sharing session was established from a source address that does not match common private network ranges. This may indicate remote access from an external or unexpected source.
securityResult.description: Screen sharing session established from an external source

securityResult.summary: A file associated with the macOS `sudo` privilege system was modified. This includes the primary sudo configuration file, files within the sudoers directory, or sudo authentication timestamp records. Such modifications may indicate changes to privilege escalation permissions or manipulation of cached sudo authentication state.
securityResult.description: Modification of sudo privilege configuration or authentication files
